name: WebApplicationCompromise
description: A case template for handling suspected or confirmed web application compromise incidents, including validation, scoping, evidence preservation, attack-path analysis, containment, remediation, provider coordination, impact assessment, and post-incident follow-up.
author: Zachary Carter
summary: Investigation of a suspected or confirmed web application compromise involving exploitation of a public-facing application, API, web server, middleware component, plugin, CMS, or related service. Scope, access path, persistence, affected data, and business impact are under investigation.

tasks.title: Validate and classify web application incident
tasks.description: Determine whether the activity represents a confirmed compromise, attempted exploitation, false positive, vulnerability scan, misconfiguration, denial-of-service condition, or other web application security event. Identify the affected application, service, or component.

tasks.title: Initiate stakeholder and provider coordination
tasks.description: Notify and coordinate with application owners, infrastructure teams, developers, DevOps, cloud teams, security leadership, legal, privacy, and any relevant hosting, CDN, WAF, or managed service providers needed to support response.

tasks.title: Identify affected applications, hosts, and environments
tasks.description: Determine the affected application, hostname, URL paths, APIs, containers, servers, databases, storage, secrets, service accounts, build pipelines, and environments such as production, staging, or development. Document confirmed and suspected scope.

tasks.title: Preserve application, host, and platform evidence
tasks.description: Preserve web server logs, application logs, API gateway logs, WAF logs, CDN logs, container logs, host artifacts, process details, deployment records, access logs, authentication logs, source snapshots, database logs, and relevant cloud telemetry needed for investigation.

tasks.title: Characterize exploitation method and access path
tasks.description: Determine how access was gained, such as SQL injection, remote code execution, authentication bypass, file upload abuse, command injection, deserialization, SSRF, credential compromise, exposed admin interface, plugin or CMS exploitation, or API abuse.

tasks.title: Assess persistence, web shells, and attacker tooling
tasks.description: Identify persistence mechanisms such as web shells, backdoors, malicious scripts, cron jobs, startup items, modified templates, unauthorized admin accounts, malicious plugins, rogue containers, or attacker tooling left on the application or supporting infrastructure.

tasks.title: Determine affected data, credentials, and downstream systems
tasks.description: Assess whether data was accessed, modified, deleted, or exfiltrated, and determine whether secrets, API keys, service credentials, session stores, databases, or downstream systems were impacted. Distinguish suspected impact from confirmed evidence.

tasks.title: Contain affected application paths and infrastructure
tasks.description: Contain the incident by blocking malicious requests, restricting vulnerable endpoints, isolating affected hosts or containers, disabling compromised accounts, removing public exposure where necessary, applying temporary WAF rules, and limiting attacker access while preserving evidence where possible.

tasks.title: Remediate vulnerabilities, malicious artifacts, and access issues
tasks.description: Remove web shells and malicious code, patch exploited vulnerabilities, rotate credentials and secrets, revoke sessions, remove unauthorized users or keys, repair insecure configurations, validate application integrity, and eliminate the original access path.

tasks.title: Assess business, legal, privacy, and regulatory impact
tasks.description: Evaluate the impact to service availability, customer trust, data confidentiality, data integrity, contractual obligations, privacy exposure, legal risk, and regulatory obligations based on the nature of the compromise and affected information.

tasks.title: Validate recovery and increase monitoring
tasks.description: Validate that the application is functioning normally after remediation, confirm malicious access has stopped, increase monitoring on affected application paths and infrastructure, and watch for reinfection, repeat exploitation, or suspicious administrator activity.

tasks.title: Implement security improvements and secure development follow-up
tasks.description: Improve secure coding controls, WAF coverage, logging, alerting, dependency management, secret handling, CI/CD security, access control, patch management, and vulnerability management based on lessons learned from the incident.

tasks.title: Document findings and close case
tasks.description: Document the affected application, exploitation path, malicious artifacts, data impact, containment actions, remediation steps, provider coordination, business impact, and lessons learned. Confirm required follow-up actions are complete before case closure.

notes.title: Initial alert or report
notes.content: Document how the possible web application compromise was identified, when it was discovered, and why the case was opened.

notes.title: Current status
notes.content: Track the current investigation phase, key updates, blockers, and next steps.

notes.title: Decision log
notes.content: Record major decisions, approvals, escalations, and significant events during the investigation.

notes.title: Stakeholders and providers
notes.content: List application owners, infrastructure teams, developers, cloud teams, legal or privacy contacts, and any involved providers or vendors.

notes.title: Affected application and components
notes.content: Document the affected web application, APIs, plugins, CMS components, libraries, and supporting services involved in the incident.

notes.title: Affected hosts, containers, and environments
notes.content: List impacted servers, containers, pods, virtual machines, storage, and environments such as production, staging, or development.

notes.title: Accounts, secrets, and service identities
notes.content: Track affected admin accounts, service accounts, API keys, certificates, tokens, secrets, and deployment identities.

notes.title: Dependencies and downstream systems
notes.content: Document related databases, external APIs, payment systems, identity providers, storage services, or other downstream systems connected to the application.

notes.title: Timeline of events
notes.content: Build a defensible timeline of relevant requests, log events, administrative actions, code or deployment changes, and suspicious activity.

notes.title: Exploitation path
notes.content: Summarize the suspected or confirmed vulnerability, exploit chain, request patterns, payloads, or access method used to compromise the application.

notes.title: Persistence and malicious artifacts
notes.content: Document web shells, modified files, malicious plugins, altered templates, rogue users, backdoors, scheduled tasks, or other persistence mechanisms.

notes.title: Authentication and administrative activity
notes.content: Record suspicious logins, privilege changes, session anomalies, unauthorized admin actions, and identity-related findings.

notes.title: Data and system impact
notes.content: Summarize evidence of data access, modification, deletion, exfiltration, credential exposure, and downstream system impact.

notes.title: Containment actions
notes.content: Document WAF rules, endpoint restrictions, host or container isolation, exposure reductions, account restrictions, and other emergency controls applied during response.

notes.title: Credential and secret actions
notes.content: Track password resets, secret rotation, key replacement, token revocation, and related access-control changes.

notes.title: Vulnerability and integrity remediation
notes.content: Summarize patches applied, code fixes, plugin updates, dependency updates, integrity validation, and removal of malicious artifacts.

notes.title: Validation of remediation
notes.content: Document how remediation was tested and how continued attacker access, reinfection, or repeat exploitation was ruled out or monitored.

notes.title: Business impact
notes.content: Document service disruption, customer-facing impact, operational effects, and reputational concerns related to the incident.

notes.title: Legal and privacy coordination
notes.content: Track guidance, decisions, and required actions involving legal, privacy, compliance, or regulatory stakeholders.

notes.title: Provider and vendor coordination
notes.content: Summarize support cases, escalations, and investigative or mitigation actions involving cloud, hosting, CDN, WAF, or software vendors.

notes.title: Notification decisions
notes.content: Record any notification obligations, rationale, recipients, and timelines where data or customer impact is involved.

notes.title: Findings summary
notes.content: Provide a concise summary of the application compromise, exploitation path, affected assets, impact, and investigative conclusions.

notes.title: Control improvements
notes.content: Document recommended improvements to secure development, dependency management, monitoring, access control, secrets management, and defensive tooling.

notes.title: Lessons learned
notes.content: Capture lessons learned, process gaps, detection gaps, and follow-up recommendations.
